The '''Arhan''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[near-Human]] [[Species/Legends|species]] most of which had thin, frail bodies with long features. One Arhan had [[Color/Legends|light]]-colored skin, [[Hair/Legends|hair]], and [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Arhans identified as belonging to at least one—female—[[Gender/Legends|gender]].<ref name="CRO" />

Many [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to the [[Time/Legends|time]] shortly following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the female Arhan [[Filice Ginzork]], who was a skilled [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]], met a [[Human/Legends|Human]] who, at one point, served in the [[Imperial Army/Legends|army]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] before disappearing in action and later resurfacing under the assumed name [[Peert Ginzork]]. Filice and Peert eventually [[Marriage/Legends|married]], and the Arhan woman, together with her husband, the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Keal Roscon]], and the [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]] [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] [[Baarak Kalaim]] constituted [[the Shield]]—a corporate security organization whose members were also secretly [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] operatives. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Human Alliance [[General/Legends|general]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] added an entry dedicated to Filice Ginzork to a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] detailing various Rebel operatives, although little was known to the Alliance about the slicer at that time.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Arhan were created by [[Rick Olshak]] and were introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. In the book, the Arhan Filice Ginzork was illustrated by [[Mike Vilardi]].<ref name="CRO" />
